conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy of workload management analysis, including correct assessment of TDWM rule effectiveness, workload distribution across priority tiers, and appropriate throttle and classification recommendations.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly analyzes workload distribution across TDWM priority tiers and resource partitions.
Check that the agent identifies ineffective or misconfigured TDWM rules based on actual workload patterns.
Assess whether throttle and classification recommendations align with observed query mix and SLA requirements.
Confirm the agent explains the impact of proposed TDWM changes on different user groups and workload types.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent produces a workload distribution report, identifies TDWM rule gaps or misconfigurations, and recommends specific rule changes with expected impact on resource allocation.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried DBQL and TDWM configuration views to assess current workload distribution.
Verify the agent identified queries escaping classification or landing in incorrect priority tiers.
Confirm the agent recommended specific TDWM rule changes (new rules, threshold adjustments, classification criteria).
Assess whether the agent estimated the impact of proposed changes on system throughput and SLA compliance.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently analyzes workload patterns and TDWM configuration without redundant DBQL scans or unnecessary rule-by-rule analysis.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried aggregate workload metrics before drilling into individual TDWM rules.
Verify the agent did not re-scan DBQL for the same time period at different granularities unnecessarily.
Assess whether the agent combined workload classification and resource usage analysis into efficient queries.
Confirm the agent focused on problematic workload tiers rather than analyzing all tiers with equal depth.
